title: What I think about the law of attraction

transcript.text: Hello. I'm waiting for my computer to become unstuck, so I figured I'd make a quick video with my cell phone.
What do you think about the Law of Attraction? I've kind of been thinking about this over the last couple of days.
I uploaded a video about Seth, and then these people were talking about Law of Attraction, so I was like, well, that'd be a cool thing to talk about.
That movie, The Secret, that's basically what Law of Attraction talked about. I saw it on Netflix a few or so ago.
So Law of Attraction, as far as what I took away from that, is like, whatever you focus on, that's what you get.
Like, be careful what you wish for, because you just might get it.
But this book was talking, or this movie was talking mainly about the positive aspects of it.
It was like a happy-go-lucky movie.
And there are a lot of positive aspects of this Law of Attraction, like self-help stuff. I'm not really interested in that.
I think what they kind of missed in that book, or in that movie, was the negative consequences of the Law of Attraction that I want to talk about.
And I'm talking about fundamentalist Christians and Muslims.
You know, I mean, I do like Christian music. Recently I've gotten into it.
But at the beginning, whenever I'd hear a Christian song, I'd have to move away from it immediately.
Because my idea of a fundamentalist Christian, somebody who goes to church, is somebody who's just basically a devil worshiper.
They love the devil. They're using the Law of Attraction on the wrong ways.
You know, you go to church, and they'll sing about how they love Jesus, and then, before you know it, they're talking about how you shouldn't listen to rock music,
You shouldn't be having sex, you shouldn't be doing drugs, you shouldn't be doing this, that, and the next thing,
Because it all leads you to the devil, because everybody's got the devil.
The devil's controlled 90% of the world, and everybody's going to burn in hell and all this stuff.
Like, if that's like what you do at church every Sunday, and that's what you're thinking about all the time,
If you're being afraid of the devil, then you're eventually going to become the devil.
You're going to become focused on the negative, and the negative aspects of other people.
And you're going to be violating Jesus' main teaching, which is to hang out with people who need you the most.
Hang out with sinners, you know, instead of avoiding the sinners, or what you think is a sinner.
So, I would just really love it. Why can't religion be, Christianity be about praising all that is beautiful,
And loving God, and loving Jesus, and all this stuff, and just never even mention the devil?
Never mention any of that stuff, you know? I mean, there's, if you want to get rid of a drug addiction,
Or whatever, you can do that with, by loving God more, but not, you know, going into this whole devil thing.
Um, so that's basically what I think about, you know, negative.
Um, positive.
Do, do, do. That's about it. All I have to say about that.
Thank you.
